purpose: Identifies when hyperscaler capex spending becomes unsustainable, triggering retrenchment
components: Threshold 1: ROIC below WACC for 4+ consecutive quarters
Kill zone requires BOTH thresholds to be crossed simultaneously
Derived from 5 historical analogues of capex cycles
current_assessment: Aggregate capex/OCF at 50-65% and approaching threshold. Meta and Amazon at or near 70%. At 30-50% capex growth rates, 2-3 hyperscalers could cross by late 2027. ROIC threshold harder to assess due to bundling.
limitations: 4 of 5 historical analogues were debt-funded — structurally different from self-funded hyperscalers
Self-funded entities may tolerate higher capex/OCF ratios
ROIC is nearly unmeasurable when AI is bundled into existing products
Prisoner's dilemma may prevent rational individual retrenchment before collective pain
